course_description: This course will provide students with foundation studies in Medical Nutrition Therapy, which underpins clinical dietetic practice. The course will commence with an introduction to the nutrition care process, nutrition screening and assessment, followed by modular, case-based workshops which cover a range of acute and chronic conditions. DIET7110 involves significant pre-preparation for classes, pre-readings and case study preparation. Students will also engage in interprofessional simulation with students from other health care disciplines.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: Students will learn the key concepts of Medical Nutrition Therapy and how to apply Medical Nutrition Therapy in professional practice.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand medical terminology and abbreviations associated with selected medical conditions and common lifestyle diseases.
Demonstrate a thorough knowledge, interpretation and application of medical nutrition therapy as an intervention in selected chronic and acute medical conditions.

Collect, analyse and interpret relevant health, medical, cultural, social, psychological, economic, personal, environmental dietary intake and food supply data in determining the health and nutritional status of individuals.
Apply the Nutrition Care Process in a client-centred manner to develop nutrition care plans.
Acknowledge the multiple factors that influence nutrition and health and apply this to practice.
Critically appraise and translate scientific knowledge and principles related to nutrition into practical information for health professionals, clients and the general public.
Work effectively within a dietetic student team in order to develop communication and process management skills, and facilitate peer-assisted, collaborative learning.

assessment_details.task_description: The purpose of this assessment is to provide you with an opportunity to apply your nutrition knowledge and skills to the Nutrition Care Process. The task will assess your skills in gathering relevant information as part of a nutrition assessment, and your ability to make appropriate nutrition diagnoses and prioritise goals. assessment_details.task_description: You will be asked to critically reflect on your experience of the interprofessional simulation. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
To be submitted via the relevant ePortfolio submission link via the course Blackboard site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
NB : If you are unable to attend your allocated IPE Simulation day for medical or extenuating circumstances , you are required to complete an absentee form and email the form as soon as possible to your course coordinator no later than two (2) calendar days after the date of the original class was held. You must submit supporting evidence along with your request.
If you are unable to attend an alternative IPE Simulation day then alternative assessment will be issued to ensure you can complete and submit a reflection piece.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.topic: Interprofessional Simulation Session Each student will be allocated and attend one Interprofessional Simulation Session during the semester. These sessions will occur on Tuesday afternoons.
Clinic placement days Each student will be allocated and attend 5 clinic days across the semester. These will be on either a Monday and/or Friday from Week 4 to Week 13.
Workshop 3 - Nutrition Diagnosis, Intervention, Monitoring and Evaluation
Workshop 5 - NCP wrap up session and practice forms
Workshop 15 - Disordered eating and mental health

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
